content.explanation:
🧱 Building Your First Real Web Page

Now that you understand how browsers read HTML, it's time to build your first real page structure.

A webpage is made of simple building blocks called tags.

📌 The 2 Most Important Tags

  • 🧭 Headings (<h1> to <h6>): Used for titles and sub-titles
  • 📝 Paragraphs (<p>): Used for normal text content

These two tags are the foundation of every website you will ever build.

🎯 Simple Rule

👉 One page = one main heading (<h1>)
👉 Everything else supports it below
content.syntax_breakdown:
🧪 How HTML is Structured

HTML works like stacking blocks from top to bottom.

You always write one complete element before starting the next.

<h1>Main Title</h1>
<h2>Sub Title</h2>
<p>This is a paragraph</p>

🧠 Hierarchy Idea

Think of it like levels in a game:

  • 🏆 h1 = Boss level title
  • 🥈 h2 = Section title
  • 🥉 h3 = Sub-section

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
Developer Profile Dashboard

Software Engineer Architecture

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Improve your page structure

1. Add an <h3> tag under the <h2>
2. The text must be: Tech Stack Focus
3. Add a paragraph under it saying: HTML Structural Layouts

👉 Then run your code and observe the hierarchy
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Example format:

Tech Stack Focus

HTML Structural Layouts

Make sure every tag is properly closed.

quiz_engine.question: What is the correct way to structure headings in HTML?
options: Mix tags randomly depending on style
Use heading levels (<h1> to <h6>) in order of importance
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 HTML headings follow a hierarchy from h1 (most important) to h6 (least important). This keeps pages structured and readable.

takeaways: Headings define structure and importance on a webpage
h1 is the main title, h2 and h3 are sub-sections

Paragraphs (

) are used for normal text content
HTML must be written in clean top-to-bottom structure

cheat_sheet.h1: Main page title (only one per page recommended)
